text: This was the president's very first event during his second term in the Rose Garden, which speaks to just how important this is to him. He got all the pomp and circumstance. He was surrounded by members of his cabinet, and of course, he brought in workers from the steel and automobile industries to make his case more. Maurice, that this is going to be what he calls the golden age of America, because his theory is that if you tack on the tariffs to imports into America, the companies here don't want to pay that, and so they are going to boost manufacturing. They're going to buy American and not want to pay the rates that you are seeing on your screen, Maurice. Now, what we did not know is exactly what countries would be impacted and the rate. And we know now that the administration used a formula to calculate what they are calling the total sum of all unfair trade practices. Now that includes currency manipulation, tariff barriers, and of course what these countries charge on American exports. So you can see here for China as the top example, sixty-seven percent. Half of that is thirty-four percent, because President Trump says he wants to be quote kind to the world, so he is not going to impose the full one hundred percent tit for tat, but fifty percent of that sum. And in addition to that, Maurice, it's important to note that what we were not expecting today was a ten percent tariff on all countries. So whether you know they are part of this worst offenders group, as the administration calls, or not, there will be a ten percent tariff. And of course, it's notable I think that he's making this announcement after the closing bell. We have been reporting for so many weeks, Maurice, about how the uncertainty of this has already rattled the stock market and certainly consumer confidence. So as we take all of this in, it'll be.
